content: Human memory’s active, curiosity-mediated updating mechanism can be formalized as a hybrid intrinsic reward framework that merges ICM’s prediction error-based surprise and RND’s state-based novelty, where episodic memory storage is gated by the same curiosity signal that drives exploration, rather than passive encoding. This gated design explains why humans remember information tied to unresolved curiosity better than random facts, and can be directly implemented in curious RL agents to improve long-horizon episodic planning in open-ended environments.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
